title: podman push fails with apparently false error that " Docker image with name "img-name" is already associated with another repository under owner "account name"'
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When pushing my first image to Github packages, it fails like this:

_Error: Error copying image to the remote destination: Error writing blob: Error initiating layer upload to /v2/company/project/myimage/blobs/uploads/ in docker.pkg.github.com: blob upload invalid: Docker image with name "myimage" is already associated with another repository under owner "company"._

The error would seem to be straightforward, but this is the first Docker image ever uploaded to our Github account, so there is nothing for it to conflict with. Also, this was the first `podman push` of the image.

On the Github side, the image seems to be in a zombie state. On the package search page, it will say there is one matching package, but provides no listing. Likewise, the related project will show that it has "one package". But if I follow the link to the package, nothing is there. 


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman push docker.pkg.github.com/company/project/myimage:1

**Describe the results you received:**

Error: Error copying image to the remote destination: Error writing blob: Error initiating layer upload to /v2/company/project/myimage/blobs/uploads/ in docker.pkg.github.com: blob upload invalid: Docker image with name "myimage" is already associated with another repository under owner "company".

**Describe the results you expected:**

Success.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Our  Github company name has uppercase characters in it. To use `podman push`, I have to provide it as all lower case. If the system mis-handles the projects case at some point, perhaps it could lead to internal inconsistency.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
2.0.2
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Host is Ubuntu 20.04 with cgroups V2 enabled.

comments.body:
I found a reference to this error here, indicating some kind of problem on the Github side:

https://github.community/t/pushing-docker-image-to-gpr-manifest-invalid-only-schema-version-2-is-supported/16913/40

I also found a "details" page for the image attempted to push on Github, indicating that the image was in fact there could be pulled:

      docker pull docker.pkg.github.com/company/project/myimage:docker-base-layer

But that didn't work either:

```
 podman pull docker.pkg.github.com/company/project/myimage:docker-base-layer
Trying to pull docker.pkg.github.com/company/project/myimage:docker-base-layer...
  manifest unknown: Docker image reference myimage:docker-base-layer not found under repo "company/project"
Error: unable to pull docker.pkg.github.com/company/project/myimage:docker-base-layer: Error initializing source docker://docker.pkg.github.com/company/project/myimage:docker-base-layer: Error reading manifest docker-base-layer in docker.pkg.github.com/company/project/myimage: manifest unknown: Docker image reference myimage:docker-base-layer not found under repo "company/project"
```

After re-running the `podman push` and `podman pull` commands a second time, they both worked.

I'm guessing that Github failed on the final steps and returned a poor error message about what happened.

I'm if there's nothing for `podman` to handle for a case like this, feel free to close.
Let's switch this to reopen if it happens again.

Happened to me as well. I created a GitHub workflow that calls "podman push" with a tag that includes the job run number. The first attempt with that job step failed, giving the error message reported here. On re-running the job (which uses the same tag a second time), the push succeeded. Running a new job (which generates a new tag) then succeeded immediately.

Maybe it's really just about the very first image push to a new repository on GitHub packages.

Log excerpt from the failing job run:

@rhatdan Could you please re-open this issue? I don't have the required permissions.

I'm using the pre-installed `podman` from GitHub's Ubuntu 20.04 image.
This is the job that succeeded on re-run: https://github.com/rolandweber/pityoulish/runs/1047389837

```txt
2020-08-30T10:22:52.9051913Z Current runner version: '2.273.0'

2020-08-30T10:22:52.9088617Z ##[endgroup]
```
Tried with podman 2.0.2 and GitHub Packages, the push/pull works fine in my case.  Have no clue why GitHub returns the error "Docker image with name..."

This is a registry-side error that I have not seen before. In case it happens again, could you share the debug logs (`podman --log-level=debug push [...]`). Before copying, please make sure to remove any credentials that may be visible in the logs.
I'd have to create a new repository to reproduce the problem. I'm a bit short on time at the moment :-(
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since this is believed to be a bad registry configuration, I am going to close.  Reopen if I am mistaken.

This keeps happening and should preferably be escalated to GitHub.

---
